But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words, and seal the book, [even] to the time of the end: many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased.

But we have still earlier indications of the existence and nature of the Apocrypha in this sense. The Book of Daniel is represented as withheld from public knowledge until the time came for its publication: xii. 4,‘ But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words and seal the book, even unto the time of the end.’ The writer of 1 Enoch speaks of his revelations as designed not for his own, i. 2, cviii. 1,…

Another comment on Then He openeth the ears of men: This verse alludes to Daniel, to whom the Holy One, blessed be He, revealed the time of redemption.9Megillah 3a. The Book of Daniel contains intimations concerning the Messianic age. By their chastisement sealeth the decree similarly refers to Daniel, for He said to him: And thou, Daniel, shut up the words and seal the book (Dan. 12:4). Another…

Then I Daniel looked, and, behold, there stood other two, the one on this side of the bank of the river, and the other on that side of the bank of the river.
And Pharoh spake with Joseph, saying, I saw in my dream, and, behold, I stood on the bank of the river.
It was at the end of two full years, that Pharaoh had a dream, and behold, he was standing on the bank of the river.
Pharaoh spoke to Yoseif, In my dream, I was standing on the bank of the river.
And [one] said to the man clothed in linen, which [was] upon the waters of the river, How long [shall it be to] the end of these wonders?

"In the OT the lifting of the hand was a part of oath-taking. Abram declined the spoils of battle saying, 'I have lifted up my hand ['I have sworn,' RSV] unto Jehovah, God most High, possessor of heaven and earth, that I will not take a thread nor a shoe-latchet nor aught that is thine' (Gen. 14: 22-23, ASV). In the Song of Moses God himself lifts up his hand to heaven in solemn oath to carry out…
And I heard the man clothed in linen, which [was] upon the waters of the river, when he held up his right hand and his left hand unto heaven, and sware by him that liveth for ever that [it shall be]…

Another thing, "Arise, O Lord." Remember your oath that the angel swore in your name, as it says (Daniel 12:7), "And I heard the man clothed in linen, who was above the waters of the river, when he held up his right hand and his left hand unto heaven, and swore by Him that lives forever." And it is written (Isaiah 30:18), "Therefore the Lord will wait, that He may be gracious unto you." And all…
And I heard, but I understood not: then said I, O my Lord, what [shall be] the end of these [things]?
And he said, Go thy way, Daniel: for the words [are] closed up and sealed till the time of the end.
Many shall be purified, and made white, and tried; but the wicked shall do wickedly: and none of the wicked shall understand; but the wise shall understand.

How can one justify the change from "white and delightsome" in 2 Nephi 30:6 to "pure and delightsome"? "White" need not refer to skin color, as is clear from the following passages from the biblical book of Daniel: "And some of them of understanding shall fall, to try them, and to purge, and to make them white, even to the time of the end: because it is yet for a time appointed" (Daniel 11:35).…
And from the time [that] the daily [sacrifice] shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, [there shall be] a thousand two hundred and ninety days.
a thousand two hundred and ninety days [a month beyond the "time, times, and a half"].
Blessed [is] he that waiteth, and cometh to the thousand three hundred and five and thirty days.
thousand three hundred and five and thirty days [forty-five days more are added].
But go thou thy way till the end [be]: for thou shalt rest, and stand in thy lot at the end of the days.
